questions.question: If you were to go in a straight line from DEPDY to ABE, what would your true course be?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 36-40: You are in a hold at DEPDY at 10,000' MSL with 5,800 LBS of jet fuel. Your destination is KLGA (21' MSL) on a true course of 052° and 45 NM away. Your alternate is KABE (394' MSL) on a true course of 277° and 72 NM from KLGA. The average winds aloft are 320° at 30 knots. Your average level flight performance is 250 KTAS and 3,600 LBS/hr of fuel burn. Your average descent performance is 1,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 1,200 LBS/hr of fuel burn. During a missed approach followed by a diversion you would climb to 15,000' MSL with climb performance of 3,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 8,000 LBS/hr of fuel burn.

questions.question: Flying inbound on a course of 205° to a VFR reporting point 24.5 kilometers away, you see another aircraft ahead of you and decide to offset. You turn 30° to the left and fly for 4 minutes. Turning back direct the VFR reporting point, your ground track is now 235°. If you maintain 115 knots the whole time and assume calm winds, how long will it take to reach the VFR reporting point?
What is your magnetic heading on the outbound leg?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 36-40. Fuel on board: 74 gallons usable. Outbound true course: 340°. Cruise: 148 KTAS, 45.4 liters per hour. Variation: 9°E. Deviation: +3°. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: Winds are forecast to be 330° at 17 knots. You are planning a flight along a true course of 205° at 112 KTAS. Variation in the area is 16° W and your compass correction is +4°. What magnetic heading should you fly?
What is the true course being flown?

questions.question: What is the magnetic heading given the following: [[NEXT_LINE]] True Course = 314°. [[NEXT_LINE]] Variation = 6° W. PA = 8500'. [[NEXT_LINE]] IAS = 142 mph. Temperature = ISA+6°C. [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind = 020° at 19 kts. [[NEXT_LINE]] Deviation = +3°.

You are flying from Tampa International (TPA) to Pensacola International (PNS) and you want to find out how many NM are between the two airports. If you know TPA is at N27°58'48" W82°31'48" and PNS is at N30°28'12" W87°10'48" then how far apart are the two airports in straight line distance?
Referencing the previous question, you decide to fly back now. What would be the true course from PNS to TPA?
questions.context: Referenced question 35: You are flying from Tampa International (TPA) to Pensacola International (PNS) and you want to find out how many NM are between the two airports. If you know TPA is at N27°58'48" W82°31'48" and PNS is at N30°28'12" W87°10'48" then how far apart are the two airports in straight line distance?

questions.question: The true course is 223°, the variation is 5° east, and the winds are from 311° at 22 knots. If your IAS is 133 mph at 4,500' MSL with an OAT of 22°C and altimeter setting of 1013 hPa, what is your groundspeed?
Find an aircraft's groundspeed given the following: Winds aloft are 335° at 18 knots, true course of 202°, and true airspeed of 174 knots.
What is your ground speed under the following conditions? [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind: 255° at 32 MPH [[NEXT_LINE]] True Course: 114° [[NEXT_LINE]] TAS: 263 km/h

You hear a radio call: "Mayday Mayday! This is N521DE, we just crashed in a field 22.2 KM northwest of Miles Municipal Airport. We need help!" If you are 41 nautical miles on the 258° radial from Miles Municipal at a ground speed of 151 MPH, how long will it be before you arrive overhead if you proceed direct to the crash site?
Departing KOSH, you are 20 NM south when you see a target on your ADS-B. There is an aircraft directly abeam your left wing 30 NM away. What is his TC and distance to KOSH?
At your time to turn you decide to fly to Cascade Fields. What will be your true course?
questions.context: Use the following information to answer questions 29 to 34: [[NEXT_LINE]] Due to deteriorating weather conditions, you are forced to file an alternate airport for your final 466 SM leg from Pine Bluffs (82V) to Sand Springs (8U1). [[NEXT_LINE]] You choose Cascade Fields (D55), which is 120° from Pine Bluffs at 272 NM. [[NEXT_LINE]] You plan for 161 KTAS on a 051° true course to Sand Springs and you burn avgas at 14.3 GPH while holding 65.4 total gallons. [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds are 310° at 31 knots.

questions.question: After the turn, how far will you travel until you reach Cascade Fields?

questions.question: You plot a course directly East from N41°16'33" W94°3'52" to N41°16'33" W94°46'3". What is the distance between these two points?
Current position is N43°59.1' W88°33.4'. Your destination is N44°04.8' W91°42.7'. How far are you from your destination?

You calculate a ground speed of 173 knots, and your G1000 shows a wind of 030° at 40 knots. What is your course, assuming your true airspeed is 142 knots and your wind correction angle is 9° to the left?
While you are tracking your true course of 347°, and the GPS shows a right crosswind component of 23 knots. If you are cruising at 93 KTAS and the variation is 7° W, what heading are you flying?
Using the information from the previous questions, what should your compass heading be on the flight to OSH? Magnetic variation is 5° W and deviation is -1°.
questions.context: Referenced question 25: You've decided to fulfill a longtime dream and fly into this year's AirVenture in Oshkosh, Wisconsin. You have faith in your single engine airplane, and have decided to fly across Lake Michigan rather than around it. Your course across the lake will be 285°, and the winds at your chosen altitude of 8,500 feet are 220° at 27 knots. Indicated airspeed is 105 knots. The temperature and pressure at 8,500 are both standard. Before you go any farther, if something goes wrong, how cold will the water below be in Fahrenheit if latest reports indicate a water temperature of 17°C? [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 26: Using the information from the previous question, and knowing your course across the lake will be 31 nautical miles, how far can you fly out over the lake before the time to continue equals the time to turn back?

questions.question: You are flying a true course of 145 and a planned true airspeed of 112 kts. If the forecast winds aloft are 240@16 what magnetic heading should you fly? The variation is 12 West and the airplane has a +4 deviation error.

A Navy aircraft tracking north is ready to return to land and the pilot must decide which of 3 nearby stationary ships to return to. One ship (Ship B) is to the right, directly abeam, 166 NM away. Ship A is in front of Ship B and Ship C is behind it. The 3 ships are equally spaced, in a staggered line, with 106.7 NM between ships. Ship C is at a magnetic bearing of 040° to Ship B and Ship A. From the aircraft, Ship A is a 071° course and Ship C is a 130° course. The winds aloft are 180° at 40 knots. The aircraft TAS is 415 knots. How far away is Ship C from the aircraft?
(Using information from the previous question) Which ship will the pilot return to in the fastest time?

choices.text: Ship B or C, the time to reach the ships are equal

questions.context: Referenced question 11: A Navy aircraft tracking north is ready to return to land and the pilot must decide which of 3 nearby stationary ships to return to. One ship (Ship B) is to the right, directly abeam, 166 NM away. Ship A is in front of Ship B and Ship C is behind it. The 3 ships are equally spaced, in a staggered line, with 106.7 NM between ships. Ship C is at a magnetic bearing of 040° to Ship B and Ship A. From the aircraft, Ship A is a 071° course and Ship C is a 130° course. The winds aloft are 180° at 40 knots. The aircraft TAS is 415 knots. How far away is Ship C from the aircraft?

questions.question: Flying through the Midwest, you are dodging building thunderstorms on the way to your destination. ATC approves a 20° left turn. Once past the weather, you turn 30° back to the right to proceed direct to your destination. At the time of your turn back on course, you've flown 128 miles. How far are you from your destination?
Currently at 15 DME and flying outbound on radial 204° from a VOR, what heading should a pilot turn to if they wish to intercept radial 230° at 23 DME?
What is the approximate distance and course from point A to point B? [[NEXT_LINE]] Point A: N34°57.7' W85°09.2' [[NEXT_LINE]] Point B: N33°37.7' W84°26.1'

choices.text: 80 NM and NE on a course of 033°
88 NM and SE on a course of 156°
67 NM and SE on a course of 145°
91 NM and SW on a course of 210°

questions.correctChoice: 88 NM and SE on a course of 156°

questions.question: A standard rate turn completes a full 360° in 120 seconds. If a plane begins a left turn on a 315° heading at standard rate, what heading will it roll out on after 0.45 minutes?
Using the above information, find magnetic heading.

Because the majority of the 79 nm flight will be over water you decide to determine when your time to continue will be equal to the time to return home. This point will be how far from KMKG?

questions.question: You just traveled 48 SM in 11 minutes 18 seconds. OAT is -5°C. Your cruise altitude is 6500 ft. MSL and you are maintaining a magnetic heading of 215°. Winds are 170@28 kts. Variation is 8W. If the altimeter setting is 30.75, what is your true course?

One evening, you are flying with a groundspeed of 120 knots, an indicated airspeed of 120 knots, OAT is -40°C, and at a pressure altitude of 7500 ft. If the winds aloft are 170° at 20 knots and the wind correction angle is to the right, what is the true course and the true heading?
